tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows markedly elevated creatinine, proteinuria, microalbuminuria, and bilirubinuria, suggesting significant renal dysfunction, likely chronic kidney disease with possible nephrotic features. Urine is abnormal in appearance and color, but no evidence of infection. Low specific gravity may indicate impaired concentrating ability. Blood glucose is normal, but HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Lipid profile is within normal limits. Recommend urgent nephrology evaluation for further assessment and management.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
Nonproliferative retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Retinopathy due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
